title: CVE Research Workflow
when_to_use: When researching known vulnerabilities in discovered services

steps.title: Search NVD
steps.tip: NVD is the authoritative source for CVE information. Bookmark it

steps.title: Search by Version
steps.instruction: Search for the technology and version (e.g., Apache 2.4.49)
steps.tip: Be version-specific. A CVE for 2.4.49 may not affect 2.4.50. Precision matters

steps.title: Analyse Each CVE
steps.instruction: For each CVE: read description, note CVSS score and vector
steps.tip: Read the full description, not just the score. The description tells you what's actually exploitable

steps.title: Check ExploitDB
steps.instruction: Check ExploitDB (exploit-db.com) for public exploit code
steps.tip: Public exploit = higher risk. No public exploit doesn't mean it's not exploitable — just that code isn't public

steps.title: Assess Attack Vector
steps.instruction: Note attack vector (Network/Local), complexity (Low/High), and impact
steps.tip: Network + Low complexity + No auth required = highest priority. All three must align for critical risk

steps.title: Check Auth Requirements
steps.instruction: Determine if authentication is required for exploitation
steps.tip: Pre-auth vulnerabilities are far more dangerous than post-auth ones. Always note this distinction

steps.title: Document Findings
steps.instruction: Document findings in a structured table for reporting
steps.tip: Table columns: CVE ID, CVSS, Description, Exploit Available, Auth Required, Priority
